description: The horn: hard growth from a living head — the ram's spiral, the ox's sweep, the deer's branching pride (antler included by extension). Growth, not weapon: Phi names what the animal grew, and has no vocabulary for what violence would make of it.
sound_symbolism: Hard 'ku' at the root, growing outward through 'ro' to settled 'na' — solidity extending, the way horn grows: slowly, from the living base.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'nolika wi kurona phelu.' (The animal has two horns.) 'kurona sui torua thuroa.' (The horn grows through the years.)

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The ram's spiral is the style's beloved logarithmic curve grown in keratin — ornament and structure in one, straight from the animal.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Cup, button, comb-stock, and signal-sound: horn was the pre-industrial plastic, harvested without harm from the herd's natural deaths.
